flags.message: This case matches the reported decision Dollar Savings Bank v. United States, 86 U.S. (19 Wall.) 227 (1874) — a pre-1925 mandatory-jurisdiction case (writ of error, no cert gate), the class the predict scope excludes. It appears to have stayed in scope because the docket row has an empty docket number, so no Term/era is parseable for the exclusion predicate. Candidate for the scope reconcile / docket-number-shape refinement.

The provisioned snapshot is a bare opinion-derived docket stub: no docket entries, no docket number, no filed/argued/terminated or cert dates. The only case-specific signal is one linked opinion cluster. The prediction therefore rests on era context and corpus base rates, not record facts; a recoverability probe of the linked cluster would likely resolve this event outright.
